articles.title: A School Stabbing: What Happened?
articles.summary: A teenage girl was stabbed at a high school called Thorpe St Andrew School near Norwich. She got minor injuries and was taken to the hospital. Police came quickly and arrested a 16-year-old boy for the stabbing. The school was put on lockdown, which means students had to stay in their classrooms for safety. The road near the school, Laundry Lane, was closed. Police say this stabbing is not connected to another problem at the school last week. Parents are worried because they haven't gotten much information about what happened. The school trust said they can't talk more right now. This is a scary event that shows why safety at school is important.

articles.title: Why Mortgage Rates Are Going Up
articles.summary: Mortgage rates in the UK are rising quickly, making it harder for people to borrow money for homes. This is the biggest change since 2022. The average rate for a two-year fixed mortgage is now above 5%, and many mortgage deals have been taken off the market. This affects people renewing their mortgages or buying their first home. The rise is linked to a war in the Middle East between the US, Israel, and Iran, which has caused oil prices to go up. Higher oil prices can lead to inflation, meaning things cost more. Lenders are increasing rates because they worry about future borrowing costs. Experts say this is bad news for borrowers and depends on how the conflict and markets change.

articles.title: New Vets Shaking Up Pet Care Costs
articles.summary: This article talks about how vet bills for pets like dogs and cats are getting very expensive. Many pet owners, like Louise who had a big bill for her dog Bow, are worried about the high costs. A group called the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is looking into why prices are rising so fast. They found that vet prices went up by 63% from 2016 to 2023. Now, more vet clinics are owned by big companies instead of small local vets. Some people think this makes prices higher, but others say it's because treatments are better and staff get paid more. The CMA wants vets to be clearer about their prices and who owns them to help pet owners. New vet businesses, like Aldi-style disruptors, might come in to offer cheaper options and change the market.

articles.title: Music from Around the World is Getting Popular on Spotify
articles.summary: Spotify, a music streaming app, says that songs in many languages are becoming more popular. English used to be the most common language in music, but now songs in Spanish, Korean, Portuguese, and other languages are in the top charts. Bad Bunny, who sings in Spanish, was the most-streamed artist in the world. Brazilian Funk, K-Pop, and Trap Latino are growing fast and earned a lot of money. English still has many popular albums, but fans are listening to more music from different countries. Spotify paid artists billions of dollars last year, helping musicians earn money from their songs.

articles.title: Why Americans Are Worried About Voting
articles.summary: A new poll shows that many Americans are feeling more worried about voting in elections. Two-thirds of people are confident that their local elections will be fair, but this number has dropped by 10 points since before the 2024 presidential election. Democrats and independents are less confident, while Republicans are a bit more confident. People are concerned about different things: some think voter fraud is a big problem, others worry about misleading information or voter suppression. President Trump is pushing for a new law that would require proof of citizenship to vote, which he says will stop fraud, but experts say fraud is very rare. The poll helps us understand how people feel about voting safety as the midterm elections approach.

articles.title: U.S. and Iran Fight with Bombs and Planes
articles.summary: The United States and Iran are fighting a war that is not stopping. On Tuesday, both sides attacked each other with bombs and said they will keep fighting. U.S. leaders say their attacks have destroyed most of Iran's ability to make nuclear fuel, which is used for dangerous weapons. President Trump also said U.S. forces hit sea mine targets in the Strait of Hormuz, a place where ships travel and Iran has threatened. In Iran, rescue workers in Tehran found a person in rubble from an airstrike, and a broken doll showed a family lived there. The U.S. Defense Secretary said the U.S. will not stop until Iran is defeated. About 140 U.S. soldiers have been hurt in the war, but over 100 are back to work. This fight is causing damage and sadness in both countries.
